tool_args.label: Summary of Evaluation table (page 23)

payload.table_title: Summary of Evaluation (Bus Priority Tools - page 23)

rows.Status: MTA and NYC DOT jointly implemented 16 SBS corridors between 2008 and 2019, out of 30 potential corridors
rows.Evaluation: Failing. While SBS outperforms the bus system overall on speed and reliability criteria, MTA and NYC DOT abandoned the program midway through implementation.

rows.Status: Since 2022, NYC DOT built only 23.1 miles of protected bus lanes, far short of the required Streets Plan target of 80 miles through 2024
rows.Evaluation: Failing. In addition to missing Streets Plan targets, NYC DOT built fewer new bus lanes in the past three years than between 2020-2021.

tool_args.label: Summary of Evaluation table (page 24)

payload.table_title: Summary of Evaluation (Bus Priority Tools - page 24)

rows.Status: 50 bus routes and over 1,000 buses are camera-enforced as of 2024
rows.Evaluation: Underway, but needs improvement to be more effective

rows.Status: DOT has installed TSP at over 2,000 intersections, meeting the Streets Plan mandate
rows.Evaluation: Met targets, but no data is reported on impact

rows.Status: Bronx redesign is complete (2023), Queens redesign will begin implementation (summer 2025), Brooklyn redesign in planning stage
rows.Evaluation: Underway, but with only modest redesign improvements

rows.Status: MTA never started this initiative outside of SBS routes

rows.Evaluation: Resounding success in reducing traffic and increasing transit ridership

payload.raw_value_text: completing just over five miles of new projects in 2024 – the lowest number in six years

tool_args.label: Congestion pricing implementation date

payload.description: MTA Central Business District Tolling Program (congestion pricing) began operation, charging most drivers $9 to enter Manhattan south of 60th Street

tool_args.label: Select Bus Service program start

payload.description: MTA and NYC DOT began planning and implementing 20 SBS corridors, starting with Bx12 SBS in the Bronx

tool_args.label: SBS program paused

payload.description: MTA announced it would put the SBS program on hold for at least three years as a cost-cutting measure

tool_args.label: Last SBS route launched (M14)

payload.description: The last SBS route, M14, launched alongside the 14th Street Busway; no additional SBS routes announced since

tool_args.label: Manhattan bus speed increase 2015-2024: 3%

payload.raw_value_text: Manhattan is the only borough where bus speeds were higher in 2024 than in 2015, increasing a modest 3% over 10 years

tool_args.label: Staten Island bus speed decrease 2015-2024: 8%

payload.raw_value_text: Staten Island buses slowed down the most of any borough, decreasing 8% between 2015 and 2024

tool_args.label: Queens bus speed decrease 2015-2024: 5%

payload.raw_value_text: Queens buses slowed down by about 5%

tool_args.label: SBS 11% faster than local, 6% faster than citywide avg

payload.raw_value_text: running 11% faster than local buses and 6% faster than the citywide average

tool_args.label: MTA estimated $315M fare evasion losses on buses 2022

payload.raw_value_text: MTA estimated $315 million of losses due to fare evasion on buses in 2022

tool_args.label: Bus fare evasion rate estimated up to 45% (2022)

payload.raw_value_text: suggesting that up to 45% of riders were not paying

tool_args.label: Bus ridership statistics - daily and annual

payload.raw_value_text: In 2024, the bus system saw an average of 1.1 million riders each day

tool_args.label: Bus ridership decline: 45% decrease from 2 million daily

payload.raw_value_text: 45% decrease from two decades ago, when the average number of daily bus trips was over 2 million

tool_args.label: Bus ridership at ~60% of 2019 levels (2024)

payload.raw_value_text: bus ridership has stagnated at about 60% of 2019 ridership levels

tool_args.label: Subway ridership at ~75% of pre-pandemic levels

payload.metric_name: ridership as percent of pre-pandemic level
payload.raw_value_text: subway ridership has rebounded to about 75% of pre-pandemic levels

tool_args.label: SBS routes 19% faster than equivalent local due to all-door boarding etc.

payload.raw_value_text: SBS routes were approximately 19% faster than equivalent local routes, due to interventions that included all-door boarding

tool_args.label: ACE - 80% of ticketed drivers never received second violation

payload.raw_value_text: 80% of drivers who received a ticket for a bus lane-related violation never received a second

tool_args.label: ACE estimated 5% speed increase

payload.raw_value_text: MTA estimates that automated enforcement resulted in average speeds increasing by 5%

payload.raw_value_text: approximately 163 miles of bus lanes throughout New York City

payload.raw_value_text: only 6.8 miles (4%) are center-running lanes

payload.metric_name: share of bus lane network that is busways
payload.raw_value_text: 5.7 miles of busways ... making up 3% of the total bus lane network

tool_args.label: Fair Fares enrollment: 360,000 enrolled, 38% of eligible

payload.raw_value_text: approximately 360,000 New Yorkers were enrolled in Fair Fares, representing just 38% of the total eligible population

tool_args.label: Fair Fares funding FY2025: $115 million

payload.raw_value_text: Funding for Fair Fares was $115 million in the FY 2025 budget, a $20 million increase from the previous year

tool_args.label: ACE - 50 bus routes camera-enforced as of 2024

payload.raw_value_text: 50 bus routes are camera-enforced, through a combination of stationary and mobile bus-mounted cameras

tool_args.label: TSP - over 2,000 intersections equipped

payload.raw_value_text: DOT has installed TSP at over 2,000 intersections, meeting the Streets Plan mandate

tool_args.label: Express bus wheelchair accessibility - 25% unable to board

payload.raw_value_text: wheelchair users were unable to board express buses 25% of the time
payload.scope: observational study by NYC Comptroller's Office

tool_args.label: Bronx redesign - 4% speed increase on most-changed routes

payload.metric_name: bus speed increase from network redesign
payload.raw_value_text: bus speeds in the Bronx had increased by 4% on the routes that received the biggest changes from the redesign

tool_args.label: Bronx redesign - up to 8% ridership increase on cross-town routes

payload.raw_value_text: ridership also increased borough-wide, growing by as much as 8% on redesigned cross-town routes

tool_args.label: Bus system size: 5,800 buses, 330 routes

payload.raw_value_text: fleet of over 5,800 buses and 330 routes across the five boroughs

tool_args.label: Local routes: 238 routes, 70% of system

payload.raw_value_text: There are 238 local routes across the city. Over 70% of New York City's bus system consists of local routes.

tool_args.label: SBS: 18 routes across 16 corridors

payload.raw_value_text: New York City's 18 SBS routes. These routes, running along 16 corridors across the city

tool_args.label: Express routes: 76 routes

payload.raw_value_text: There are 76 express routes, 29 of which are in Staten Island and 30 in Queens

tool_args.label: Express bus one-way fare: $7

payload.raw_value_text: one-way fares set at $7

tool_args.label: 14th Street Busway - permanent extension

payload.description: Better Buses Restart extended and made permanent the 14th Street Busway. Travel speeds increased up to 47% after installation in 2019, making trips 10 minutes faster.

tool_args.label: Better Buses Action Plan (2018)

payload.description: Set goals of increasing bus speeds by 25% citywide, installing 10-15 miles of new bus lanes per year, installing TSP at hundreds of intersections, and piloting new busways and automated enforcement

tool_args.label: Better Buses Restart initiative

payload.description: 2021 NYC DOT initiative to install nine new major bus lanes and busways throughout the city. Extended and made permanent the 14th Street Busway and created plans to implement five additional busways.

tool_args.label: NYC Streets Plan (2021)

payload.description: Passed by City Council in 2021. Mandates NYC DOT install 20 miles of protected bus lanes in 2022 and 30 miles each year through 2026, and implement TSP at thousands of intersections by 2026.

tool_args.label: Bronx Bus Network Redesign (update from Behind Schedule report)

payload.description: First major update to Bronx bus network since 1950s. Major changes to 13 out of 46 routes, added 2 new routes, removed 258 bus stops. Excludes express buses. Resulted in 4% speed increase on most-changed routes and up to 8% ridership increase on cross-town routes.

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign

payload.description: Finalized early 2025. Includes simplifying, straightening, and consolidating routes, $30 million of new/increased service, new 'rush' limited routes. Set to go into effect June 2025. Did not include new SBS routes.

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign

payload.description: Currently in planning stages. Draft plan contains proposal for new SBS route between Kensington, Brooklyn and JFK Airport (B55 SBS). Final plan expected in second half of 2025.

tool_args.label: Fair Fares program

payload.description: City-funded program offering NYC residents with household incomes at 145% of FPL a 50% discount on subway, local, and SBS bus fares. Launched 2019. 360,000 enrolled (38% of eligible). FY2025 budget $115M. Discount does not apply to express buses.

tool_args.label: OMNY fare payment system

payload.description: Contactless fare payment system launched in 2019 to replace MetroCards. By end of 2024, 60% of transit riders used OMNY. Anticipates completing rollout and retiring MetroCards by 2026. Includes automatic fare-capping.

tool_args.label: MTA 2018 Bus Plan

payload.description: Proposed redesigning citywide bus network, implementing all-door boarding, improving bus dispatching, and making street design and traffic signal changes on select corridors in coordination with NYC DOT. Spearheaded by then-NYC Transit President Andy Byford.

tool_args.label: SBS on-time performance rate 75%

payload.raw_value_text: SBS buses have the best on-time performance, with a rate of 75%

tool_args.label: Express bus on-time performance rate 63%

payload.raw_value_text: express buses have an on-time performance rate of 63%

tool_args.label: 15 of 18 worst on-time lines are express buses

payload.claim_text: 15 of the 18 bus lines that arrive at their stops on-schedule less than half of the time are express buses
payload.description: Express buses dominate among worst-performing routes by on-time performance

tool_args.label: SBS program: 16 of 20 proposed corridors implemented

payload.claim_text: NYC DOT and MTA have failed to commit to the program, implementing just 16 out of 20 proposed corridors
payload.description: SBS program fell short of original 20-corridor plan

tool_args.label: Streets Plan bus lane mandates missed: 27.6 of 50 miles (2022-2023)

payload.metric_name: bus lane miles built vs mandate
payload.raw_value_text: DOT has built or upgraded only 27.6 miles of bus lanes out of the 50 miles required between 2022 and 2023

tool_args.label: 13.4% of disabled New Yorkers rely on buses to commute

payload.raw_value_text: 13.4% of disabled New Yorkers report relying on buses to commute to work

tool_args.label: 5.6% of bus commuters have disabilities vs 3.6% of subway

payload.raw_value_text: 5.6% of all bus commuters are people with disabilities, compared to 3.6% of subway riders

tool_args.label: 1.2 million wheelchair/mobility device rides on local buses (2023)

payload.metric_name: wheelchair/mobility device rides on local buses
payload.raw_value_text: Wheelchair and mobility device users ... took over 1.2 million rides on local buses in 2023

tool_args.label: Reduced-Fare Program: 1.5 million enrolled

payload.raw_value_text: over 1.5 million New Yorkers enrolled in the program

tool_args.label: TSP evaluation 2017: up to 18% travel time reduction

payload.raw_value_text: a 2017 evaluation found that TSP could reduce bus travel times by an average of 18% on the five SBS routes where it was active

tool_args.label: 14th Street Busway: speeds up 47%, trips 10 min faster

payload.raw_value_text: Travel speeds on 14th Street in Manhattan increased up to 47% after the installation of a dedicated busway in 2019, making trips for bus riders on the corridor 10 minutes faster

tool_args.label: Busway speed improvements of 30% or more where implemented

payload.raw_value_text: Dedicated busways ... have sped up buses by 30% or more where implemented

tool_args.label: Recommendation: Expand Fair Fares to 200% FPL

payload.claim_text: Expand Fair Fares to New Yorkers with incomes at 200% of the federal poverty level and CUNY students, to reach more regular commuters and transit users
payload.description: Report recommendation to expand Fair Fares eligibility threshold from 145% to 200% of FPL

tool_args.label: Fair Fares expansion estimate: $150M/year at 200% FPL

payload.raw_value_text: A study by the Community Service Society of New York estimates that such an expansion would cost approximately $150 million per year, $35 million more than current spending levels

tool_args.label: Fair Fares expansion 2025: 145% FPL threshold

payload.description: City expanded Fair Fares eligibility to 145% of FPL, making another 200,000 people eligible

tool_args.label: Student OMNY Cards: 500k-600k eligible students

payload.raw_value_text: Approximately 500,000 to 600,000 K-12 students are eligible for student OMNY passes

tool_args.label: Table 1: Average speed by service (2024)
23.1 miles protected bus lanes built 2022-2024 vs 80-mile target

payload.raw_value_text: Since 2022, NYC DOT built only 23.1 miles of protected bus lanes, far short of the required Streets Plan target of 80 miles through 2024

tool_args.label: Local bus average speed 7.8 mph

payload.raw_value_text: Local buses are the slowest bus service in the city, averaging speeds of 7.8 mph citywide

tool_args.label: Bus speed increase of 8.4% on routes with new bus lanes (2019)

payload.metric_name: bus speed increase from new bus lanes
payload.raw_value_text: MTA reports that bus speeds increased by 8.4% on routes where NYC DOT added bus lanes in 2019
